visual_notes: Ruby is the red variety of corundum — the intensity of Cr3+ fluorescence in daylight gives Burmese rubies an inner glow that red garnets and spinel cannot replicate. In strong sunlight, a fine Burmese ruby appears to emit light. The six-rayed star in star ruby is one of the most captivating optical effects in gemology.
prompt_fragments: pigeon's blood ruby — chromium fire glowing in Mogok marble
star ruby cabochon with six-rayed silk asterism
vivid red fluorescent corundum — blood of gems
vibe_notes: Ruby holds the deep-red that human eyes are tuned to desire — the color of blood, roses, and fire condensed into Mohs-9 corundum. In strong daylight, Burmese pigeon's blood ruby appears to glow from within — the chromium fluorescence adding spectral energy that no mere pigment can produce.
folklore_summary: Ruby is the 'king of gems' — a title given in Sanskrit texts that has persisted across cultures. Burmese warriors inserted rubies under their skin before battle believing it would make them invincible.
